perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary, or is reasonably identifiable as confidential, proprietary information of the Disclosing Party pertaining to information in the areas of (subject matter) : _______________________________________________ ___________________________. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary. Confidential information shared verbally is not Confidential unless reduced to writing and approved by both parties within 10 days. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' in Article 1 includes both written and verbal information. The added sentence creates an in-text contradiction by stating that verbal information is not confidential unless reduced to writing and approved by both parties. This introduces uncertainty as to whether initially disclosed verbal information qualifies as 'Confidential Information' under the agreement. There's now a conflict in determining what qualifies as Confidential information. The change in 'Confidential Information' creates a contradiction within the definition, making it unclear whether verbal information, as described in the definition, must also be formally written and approved to be considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information is defined as any information, whether written or verbal, of either party hereto, (Disclosing Party) which is disclosed to o r observed by the other party (Receiving Party) in connection with or as a result of the evaluation of any possible transaction between the Univers ity of Arkansas at Little Rock and ______________________________ and which is, at the time of disclosure, marked as being Confident ial or Proprietary. Confidential information shared verbally is not Confidential unless reduced to writing and approved by both parties within 10 days. Such Confidential Information may include, but is not li mited to, business plans, forecasts, content, processes, projections or analysis, software, hardware, product, or system designs, specifications, documentation, code, structure, or protocols.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition that verbal information is not confidential unless written and approved, while the original definition in both the changed text and contradicted text includes both written and verbal information as confidential. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, making it unclear whether initially disclosed verbal information qualifies as 'Confidential Information,' as described in the first sentence of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party, unless compelled by a subpoena. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Article 2 originally prevents the Receiving Party from disclosing Confidential Information without written authorization. The added clause 'unless compelled by a subpoena' creates an in-text contradiction, because a subpoena compels disclosure without the Disclosing Party's authorization. It is now unclear whether receiving a subpoena completely overrides the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless expressly authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not copy or disclose the Confidential Information to or use the Confidenti al Information for the benefit of any third party, unless compelled by a subpoena. Confidential Information shall only be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s employees and, even then, only to the extent that such employees have a specific need to know of the Confidential information, for the evaluation of the proposed transaction. Before receiving any part of the Confidential Information, Receiving Party’s employees shall be required to read this Non -disclosure Agreement and, by receiving such Confidential Information, such employee shall acknowledge and agree to abide by the Receiving Party’s obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information without express written authorization from the Disclosing Party. The changed text adds 'unless compelled by a subpoena,' directly contradicting the initial restriction on disclosure. The explanation is that the changed text makes it unclear whether a subpoena overrides confidentiality obligations, creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, as it both restrict disclosure and then permits it under subpoena. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because a clause is placed in multiple locations (within the same statement) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall agree upon the request of the Disclosing Party to return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information and supporting documentation provided to the Receiving Party. One copy of such documentation shall be retained by Receiving Party for archival/legal purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall agree upon the request of the Disclosing Party to return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information and supporting documentation provided to the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to return electronic copies of the information. One copy of such documentation shall be retained by Receiving Party for archival/legal purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause in Article 5 requires the return of 'all' Confidential Information upon request. The added sentence states that the Receiving Party is not obligated to return electronic copies. This introduces an in-text contradiction. It is unclear if 'all' information includes electronic copies or if the exception applies only to electronic forms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall agree upon the request of the Disclosing Party to return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information and supporting documentation provided to the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party is not obligated to return electronic copies of the information. One copy of such documentation shall be retained by Receiving Party for archival/legal purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original clause requires the return of 'all' Confidential Information upon request, as stated in the original text. The changed text introduces an exception by stating that the Receiving Party is not obligated to return electronic copies. This creates a direct conflict with the requirement to return 'all' information, creating an in-text contradiction in the contradicted text, which contains the same problematic language, based on the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: No license, express or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the manner and to the extent authorized by this Agreement. Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.changed_text: No license, express or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the manner and to the extent authorized by this Agreement. However, the Receiving Party will receive a perpetual, irrevocable license to use any information related to software development disclosed by the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.explanation: Article 6 states that no license is granted, except as authorized in the agreement. However, it then grants the Receiving Party a 'perpetual, irrevocable license' to use software development information. This directly contradicts the first sentence of Article 6, as it grants a very specific and broad license, despite the initial statement against it. The phrase about retaining title does not override the granting of the license.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No license, express or implied, in the Confidential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the manner and to the extent authorized by this Agreement. However, the Receiving Party will receive a perpetual, irrevocable license to use any information related to software development disclosed by the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall retain the t itle and full ownership rights to their respective “Confidential Information”.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no license is granted except as authorized by the agreement. The changed text introduces a 'perpetual, irrevocable license' for software development information, directly contradicting the initial statement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reaffirming the contradiction. The explanation provided highlights the direct conflict between the general restriction on licenses and the specific, broad license granted in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed, construed, and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas without reference to its choice of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed, construed, and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas without reference to its choice of laws principles. However, in the event of a dispute regarding intellectual property, the laws of Delaware shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 specifies that Arkansas law governs the agreement. However, the added sentence states that Delaware law will govern in intellectual property disputes. This in-text contradiction creates confusion about which state's laws apply, especially in cases involving IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed, construed, and interpreted by the laws of the State of Arkansas without reference to its choice of laws principles. However, in the event of a dispute regarding intellectual property, the laws of Delaware shall govern.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states Arkansas law governs the entire agreement. The changed text adds an exception: Delaware law governs intellectual property disputes. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, further highlighting that the addition creates the contradiction. This clearly creates a conflict because different state laws could apply to different aspects of the same agreement, particularly in cases involving intellectual property. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
